chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Misc. Appln. No.S-329 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection 2. For order on MA-4945/2022 3. For hearing of main case. 08-09-2023 None present for applicant. = ARSHAD HUSSAIN KHAN, J.- Through instant Criminal Miscellaneous Application, the applicant has impugned the Order dated 18.04.2022 passed by learned IInd Additional Sessions Judge/Ex- Officio Justice of Peace, Hyderabad, whereby the application u/s 22-A 6(iii) Cr.P.C filed by the present applicant was dismissed. 2. It is the case of the applicant that private respondents were selling the piece of land which was purchased by them from the father of applicant to the strangers and are issuing him threats. The applicant by way of making an application u/s 22-A-6(iii) Cr.P.C sought for issuance of direction to SHO concerned to provide protection but his application was dismissed by learned Ex-officio Justice of Peace, which is impugned by the applicant before this Court in the present proceedings. 3. Since filing of this application the applicant and his counsel have not come before the Court. However, record of the case has been perused, which reflects that there is dispute between the parties over landed property which is purely of a civil nature case for which the learned trial Court while passing the impugned order dated 18.04.2022 2 has directed the parties to approach the competent civil Court for redressal of their grievances, which direction appears to be reasonable. No illegality has been committed by the learned trial Court while passing the impugned order, hence, the same cannot be interfered.

However, record of the case has been perused, which reflects that there is dispute between the parties over landed property which is purely of a civil nature case for which the learned trial Court while passing the impugned order dated 18.04.2022 2 has directed the parties to approach the competent civil Court for redressal of their grievances, which direction appears to be reasonable. No illegality has been committed by the learned trial Court while passing the impugned order, hence, the same cannot be interfered. Consequently, this application being meritless is dismissed. JUDGE Shahid
